rationale.R1.1: The section is structured around a gap statement ('current consensus is wrong') and a contribution claim ('first first-order method at O(log(1/ε))'). Reviewing requires assessing whether the thesis is coherently set up and the contribution is precisely scoped.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple chained logical claims are present: noise-variance-scales-with-d → O(1/d²) step bounds → slowdowns → practitioners abandon SGD. Each link must hold. The conclusion that CA-SGD 'obviates momentum' also requires the prior chain to be internally consistent.
rationale.R1.3: The section asserts convergence rates (O(log(1/ε))), cost equivalences ('at first-order cost'), and proof technique claims ('standard Lyapunov arguments') without showing theorems or experiments. Review must flag which claims are asserted vs. demonstrated here.
rationale.R1.4: The delegation's most load-bearing responsibility: 'first first-order method to achieve this rate without large noise injection' is a strong priority claim. Failure to check this against escape literature (e.g., perturbed SGD, NEON2, cubic regularization) would invalidate the review.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact characterizes a 'current consensus' and 'prevailing view' without a single citation. Review must assess whether these characterizations of prior work are accurate and properly attributed.
